q: An optical instrument used for viewing distant objects is called a telescope.
Two types of telescope are the astronomical telescope and the terrestrial telescope.
The refracting astronomical telescope consists of an objective of long focal length and an eyepiece of short focal length.
Both lenses in a refracting astronomical telescope are convex lenses.
The final image formed by the eyepiece in an astronomical telescope is real and erect.
To obtain sharp images of distant objects, the objective of an astronomical telescope should have a large diameter.
Most telescopes used at astronomical observatories use a convex lens as the objective.
The reflecting telescope was first suggested by Newton.
In the terrestrial telescope, a concave lens is used as an eyepiece to produce an erect final image.
Total reflecting prisms are used in binoculars to reduce the length of the terrestrial telescope.
Binoculars consist of a pair of telescopes, one for each eye.
